developer.comment: Built and maintained by OpenAI; repo under github.com/openai.

first-release.comment: Open-sourced April 2025 (launched ~Apr 16, 2025); rewritten from TypeScript to Rust in mid-2025. Checked 2026-06-02.

status.comment: Actively developed (frequent releases); generally available, not labeled beta.

license.comment: The CLI agent itself is Apache-2.0 licensed. License of the agent, not the model.

specialization.comment: Broad coding agent for writing, refactoring, debugging across codebases.

website.comment: Official Codex CLI docs; repo at github.com/openai/codex.

interface.comment: Codex CLI is the terminal product; OpenAI also ships a separate IDE extension and Codex Cloud, but this candidate is the CLI.

host-ides.comment: Runs in the terminal; the CLI form factor does not run inside an IDE (the IDE extension is a separate product).

open-source.comment: Agent source is openly licensed (Apache-2.0), predominantly Rust.

default-model-provider.comment: Default/flagship models are OpenAI's GPT (e.g. GPT-5.x); can be pointed at others via custom providers.

model-choice.comment: Mid-session model switching via /model; supports configurable custom model providers.

bring-your-own-key.comment: Can authenticate with an OpenAI API key (billed at API rates) or configure custom providers with env_key.

local-model-support.comment: Built-in support for local models via Ollama and LM Studio (--oss flag / oss_provider; reserved provider IDs include ollama and lmstudio).

multi-file-editing.comment: Reads, edits, and applies coordinated changes across files in a task.

autonomous-task-execution.comment: Chains edit/run/test steps toward a goal; supports Full Access mode and non-interactive exec for end-to-end runs.

terminal-command-execution.comment: Runs shell commands as part of its loop, under approval/sandbox modes.

mcp-support.comment: Documented MCP support (STDIO and streamable HTTP servers) configured in ~/.codex/config.toml or via codex mcp.

subagents-parallel-agents.comment: Subagent workflows spawn specialized agents in parallel (fan-out up to 6 threads by default) and consolidate results.

codebase-indexing.comment: No built-in semantic index/embeddings store; it reads files on demand into context. Semantic indexing requires third-party tools.

planning-mode.comment: Has a /plan slash command and explains plans before executing; Read-only approval mode supports plan-first work.

skills.comment: Agent Skills package instructions/resources/scripts loaded on demand; also custom slash commands.

multimodal-input.comment: Accepts image input (screenshots, design specs) via the -i flag.

browser-computer-use.comment: The CLI has web search but no documented ability to drive a real browser/GUI to act and verify. Computer-use features appear in the separate Codex App, not the CLI; marked false for the CLI.

operating-mode.comment: Interactive TUI with approval workflows (supervised), Full Access autonomous runs, and codex cloud / exec for async/background; no inline tab completion.

inline-completion.comment: Terminal agent with no as-you-type tab autocomplete (typical for CLI agents).

background-async-execution.comment: Supports codex cloud task delegation and non-interactive exec for scripted/background runs.

persistent-memory.comment: Session resume restores prior conversation with repo context; AGENTS.md provides durable per-project guidance across sessions.

checkpoints-rollback.comment: No built-in single-action revert of an agent run in the core CLI; OpenAI recommends using your own git checkpoints to revert. Marked false per guideline that manual git does not count.

git-integration.comment: Git-aware: surfaces a transcript of actions, can review against branches/commits (/review) and work within the git workflow.

pr-creation.comment: PR review/creation via GitHub integration and Codex Cloud/GitHub Actions workflows; CLI can run commands to push/open PRs.

custom-rules-instructions.comment: Reads AGENTS.md (and .codex/ layers) walked up from the working directory for project-level guidance.

hooks-extensibility.comment: Programmable lifecycle hooks (hook browser, user-prompt hook), plus MCP servers, custom slash commands, and a Python SDK.

permission-model.comment: Three approval modes (Auto, Read-only, Full Access) plus sandboxed execution; user configures the posture.

self-host-on-prem.comment: Open-source CLI runs locally on the user's machine and can be pointed at fully local/self-hosted models (Ollama, LM Studio); using OpenAI's hosted models is the default path.

privacy-mode.comment: OpenAI does not train on Business/Enterprise/API inputs by default; Zero Data Retention available for eligible orgs. Consumer Free/Plus default may differ.

enterprise-controls.comment: Via ChatGPT Enterprise: SSO/SCIM, RBAC, admin/workspace controls and compliance/audit capabilities.

pricing-model.comment: Codex included in ChatGPT Free, Go ($8), Plus ($20), Pro ($200) etc.; API-key mode is usage-based per token. CLI itself is free/open-source.

free-tier.comment: Codex is included in the ChatGPT Free plan for quick coding tasks; CLI is free to install (open-source).

entry-price-usd-mo.comment: Genuine free tier covers core use (Codex on ChatGPT Free). Cheapest paid plan with higher limits is Go at $8/mo, then Plus at $20/mo; API-key usage billed per token.

maturity.comment: Widely deployed and polished (millions of users, frequent releases) but APIs/UX still evolve rapidly; ~1 year old. Not yet a 5 due to fast-moving surface.

community-size.comment: ~80k+ GitHub stars and millions of monthly users; among the largest CLI agents alongside Claude Code. Checked 2026-06-02.

extension-plugin-ecosystem.comment: Growing ecosystem of MCP servers, skills, subagents and plugins (e.g. 150+ curated tools); supports MCP and custom skills/commands.
